title: Constitutional capability model for autonomous in-pi orchestration — config legislative, main-agent executive, validators judicial; capability mutation requires human ratification
context: Evaluation (2026-05-24) of whether the orchestrator->subagent operational pipeline (run this session by a Claude-Code orchestrator tasking subagents) is achievable by an extensions-tools-constrained in-pi agent using pi-context + pi-jit-agents. Finding: the declarative pipeline is ~85% expressible today (pi-workflows agent/command/gate/block/monitor/pause steps; shell IS available via command/gate steps; substrate authorship complete via Cluster A+B; introspect.agentContract ALREADY projects an AgentSpec to a tool shape). The genuinely missing pieces for ADAPTIVE self-orchestration are (1) agent-dispatch as a first-class tool (FEAT-004 / DEC-0044, open; pi-jit-agents is tool-less) and (2) operation-granular JIT capability composition + sandbox (FEAT-005). User articulated the governing model: a subagent is a programmatic action (a tool call carrying composed atomic-context chunks) that receives its OWN exactly-scoped tools+perms, JIT-composed from an empty-state registry (no ambient tools/perms; configs+schemas+macros materialize everything — generalizing DEC-0015/0011/0025). The human stays in the loop by AUTHORIZING the config and its mutations proposed by the main agent. Framed as a separation of powers ('constitutional').
decision: ENACTED (2026-05-26), in code terms (the prior legislative/executive/judicial framing was thinking-scaffolding; the model below is the operative form). No genuine fork remained at settlement — the model is determined by FEAT-005 + DEC-0014/0015/0011 + FGAP-102; settling = recording it. (1) A dispatched agent's tools are declared in its .agent.yaml spec (schema-shaped, macro/template-composed at the jit-agents compile boundary); default EMPTY (DEC-0015/0011 generalized) — an agent gets nothing it does not declare. (2) The grant is OPERATION-GRANULAR, not tool-wholesale (FEAT-005): a scoped right (run `npm test`; write path X) rather than `bash`/`write` entire. config + schemas + macros are the registry of what is grantable (default empty); the spec carries the per-invocation scoped grant drawn from it. (3) The dispatch path grants exactly the spec's declaration, CLAMPED to a subset of the dispatching parent's grant AT OPERATION SCOPE (FGAP-099, enforced at the DEC-0044 dispatch extension). A child can never exceed its parent; subagents are spec-blind recipients with no escalation path. (4) The orchestrator (DEC-0014: tools-only, no ambient bash/read/write/edit) is the SOLE spec/schema author. It may author work-orders/specs whose grants are within the existing registry (writer.kind=agent — allowed). WIDENING the registry (a new tool/op/capability bundle) is a capability mutation requiring HUMAN ratification: writes to capability/registry fields require writer.kind=human; agent-authored capability expansion is rejected at the block-api write chokepoint (no parallel ungated path). The existing DispatchContext WriterIdentity discriminant (human|agent|monitor|workflow) is the lever. (5) The TERMINAL acceptance verdict on any executable artifact (code or substrate change) is produced by RUNNING THE REAL DETERMINISTIC CHECKS the executive cannot fake — compiler/type-check/test exit code, schema validator, git diff, real runtime events — never an LLM self-assessment (FGAP-102; DEC-0018 is its executable form, inherited by autonomous loops per FEAT-006). An LLM may triage/route but is never the terminal gate. This is the governance layer over FEAT-004 (dispatch) + FEAT-005 (capability composition); homed at the DEC-0044 dispatch extension; enabled by DEC-0014. The only build-time residue is the literal JSON schema of a scoped grant (a FEAT-005 implementation detail, not a decision).

options_considered.label: Constitutional capability model (CHOSEN direction)
options_considered.description: Config legislative + main-agent executive + validators judicial; empty-state default; capability mutation human-ratified as a structural gate.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Makes self-orchestration TRUSTWORTHY (minimal, exact, sanctioned authority per subagent) rather than merely possible. Cost: requires building FEAT-004 dispatch-as-tool + FEAT-005 capability-composition/sandbox + a structural ratification gate; more upfront machinery than ad-hoc dispatch.

options_considered.label: Bare FEAT-004 dispatch without a capability layer
options_considered.description: Agents-as-tools with category-granular tool grants only (--tools read), no per-operation perms, no ratification gate.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Simpler; but lets a self-orchestrating main agent grant broad authority to subagents (edit source, run shell) without exact scoping or human sanction.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Necessary but not sufficient — unsafe for autonomous self-orchestration; the capability + constitutional layer is the property that makes it trustworthy.

consequences: Gates/shapes FEAT-004 (dispatch-as-tool) and FEAT-005 (JIT capability composition + sandbox) — both must land for the executive branch to function autonomously.
FGAP-063 (procedural-policy has no substrate block) is the likely legislative home for the process/capability canon — this DEC motivates closing it.

Converts human-authorization from behavioral norm (mandate-001 / substrate-authorship-semantics) to a structural gate on legislative (config/capability) mutation; the executive proposes, the human ratifies.

Builds on DEC-0014 (tools-only main thread — no ambient authority), DEC-0015/0011/0025 (empty-state config-driven), DEC-0019/0020 (dual-surface), DEC-0018 (judicial: runtime-demo+adversarial). introspect.agentContract already supplies the agent->tool-shape projection the executive needs.

The declarative pipeline remains expressible as a pi-workflow today; this model is what enables the ADAPTIVE (replan-on-finding) orchestration a static DAG cannot express, by giving the reasoning main agent safe dispatch + capability tools.

references.label: workflow shell-capable step types (command/gate) — pipeline expressibility
